hook.text: You can't manage what you can't see. Kanban boards make invisible work visible.

buildup.text: Columns represent stages: To Do, In Progress, Review, Done. Cards represent work items.

discovery.text: When everything is on the board, the team sees bottlenecks, blockers, and imbalances instantly.

twist.text: The board doesn't tell people what to do — it shows reality. The team decides what to fix.

climax.text: A shared visual language replaces status meetings. The board speaks for itself.

punchline.text: The best status report is a wall full of sticky notes.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of a Kanban board?
options: Making invisible work visible so teams can spot problems
